data.title: What's Going On With PayPal Stock Today?
data.description:
Stock Drop And Scrapped Outlook

The stock fell 20.31% on February 3, 2026, dropping from $52.33 to $41.70 after the company revealed the issues and scrapped its long-term outlook. The lead plaintiff deadline is April 20, 2026.

At its February 2025 Analyst/Investor Day, PayPal projected 8% to 10% total payment volume growth by 2027, along with 7% to 9% transaction-margin growth and low-teens EPS expansion.

However, in February 2026, the company reported results that contradicted those targets, citing weaker Branded Checkout performance, operational challenges, competitive pressures, and macroeconomic headwinds. It also announced the termination of CEO James Alexander Chriss.

Venmo Expands Rewards Offering

Separately, PayPal-owned Venmo expanded its Stash rewards program, allowing users to earn up to 5% cash back on everyday Venmo purchases at select merchants.

The program now includes Venmo checkout transactions within selected reward bundles, with monthly cash back capped at $100.

Venmo is also expanding its merchant network, adding Sephora, Ulta, Taco Bell, and Pizza Hut, while its debit card and checkout products continue to see double-digit growth in transaction volume and monthly active accounts.

"Venmo has always been where money moves between people, and now it is where millions choose to spend," said Alexis Sowa, General Manager of Venmo at PayPal.

Venmo's Role In PayPal's Ecosystem

Venmo, owned by PayPal, positions itself as a social payments platform offering tools to send, split, shop, and sell, serving millions of users across the United States.

PYPL Technical Analysis

PayPal is currently trading within its 52-week range, which spans $79.50 to $38.46. The stock is trading 6.4% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) but is 6.4% below its 100-day SMA, suggesting mixed short-term momentum. Additionally, it is 20.4% below its 200-day SMA, indicating a bearish long-term trend.

The relative strength index (RSI) is 63.75, which is considered neutral, suggesting the stock is neither overbought nor oversold.

  • Key Resistance: $51.67 — A level where selling pressure may emerge.
  • Key Support: $43.50 — A critical level for buyers to defend.

PYPL Upcoming Earnings

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 5, 2026 (confirmed) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: $1.28 (Down from $1.33)
  • Revenue Estimate: $8.09 Billion (Up from $7.79 Billion)
  • Valuation: P/E of 9.2x (Indicates value opportunity)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $67.88. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Mizuho: Downgraded to Neutral (Lowers Target to $50.00) (April 16)
  • Citigroup: Neutral (Raises Target to $48.00) (April 8)
  • Loop Capital: Initiated with Hold (Target $46.00) (March 31)

PYPL Price Action: PayPal Holdings shares were up 0.20% at $49.67 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo: Michael Vi on Shutterstock.com

data.title: Senator Goes Stock Shopping: Here's His Two Magnificent 7 Picks For 2026
data.description:
Stock transactions made by members of Congress continue to be closely followed by retail investors. While some transactions stand out due to the size of the company or committee assignments, other transactions are monitored due to their timing and the stock performance.

• Microsoft stock is gaining positive traction. What’s driving MSFT shares up?

Senator Goes Magnificent Seven Shopping

Sen. John Boozman (R-Ark.) recently disclosed the buying and selling of several stocks and ETFs as reported by the Benzinga Government Trades page.

Boozman last purchased Nvidia stock back in August 2025. The senator also bought the stock in January, February and May of 2025, making this the fifth purchase since the start of 2025 with no sales.

Microsoft shares have been bought more times by Boozman since the start of 2025 and with the two purchases this year, this could signal the bullishness on the technology company by Boozman.

Microsoft’s Worst Performing Magnificent Seven Stock

The purchase by Boozman comes with Microsoft stock trailing the market and other Magnificent Seven names.

Here are the current year-to-date returns:

  • Amazon.com: +9.6%
  • Alphabet Inc: +7.1%
  • Nvidia: +5%
  • Meta: +3.7%
  • Apple: -2.9%
  • Tesla: -11.3%
  • Microsoft: -11.5%

Compared to the 2.6% gain of the S&P 500, Microsoft is significantly underperforming in 2026.

Boozman is one of the largest buyers and sellers of stocks among Congress members based on trading volume. Quiver Quantitative estimates that Boozman had $1.87 million in stock and ETF transactions in 2025 and has already passed $590,000 in trades in 2026.

Photo: W. Scott McGill via Shutterstock

data.title: Quantum Stocks Are Booming — S&P Global Says 2026 'Is Triggering Change'
data.description:
• XNDU stock is racing higher. See the chart and price action here.

Quantum stocks have surged across the board.

‘Quantum Has Been Catalyzed' 

Traders read Nvidia's launch of the Ising family of open-source AI models as a sector-wide catalyst rather than just a technical update — and the macro backdrop reinforced that bet.

An S&P Global 451 Research report found that global quantum investment surpassed $55 billion in 2025, with market revenue projected to jump from roughly $2.5 billion in 2025 to nearly $9 billion in 2026. 

"Only a few months into 2026, the quantum computing industry has been catalyzed: M&A activity is surging, investment continues to grow … and deployment and commercial conversations are increasingly supplanting hypotheticals," the analysts wrote.

The report also noted that 76% of enterprise respondents now believe quantum computing will deliver material value within five years.

"2025 ignited interest. 2026 is triggering change," S&P Global analysts wrote.

Quantum computing is no longer a lab-stage curiosity — and this week’s price action suggests Wall Street is starting to price that in.

Photo: Dragon Claws / Shutterstock

data.title: Best Quantum Stock? Citron Research Says Nvidia CEO Just Told Investors Which Name To Watch
data.description:
Best Quantum Stock

"Don't listen to Citron. Listen to Jensen," Citron Research tweeted Thursday.

Citron said that Nvidia has laid out "exactly who their partners are" when it comes to quantum.

"$INFQ is the only company Nvidia selected twice for both calibration and decoding, yet trades at half the market cap of $RGTI, a company Nvidia didn't select al all. $INFQ deserves to be twice the market cap of $RGTI. Not the other way around.

Citron said that this is the "most obvious mispricing in the quantum market right now."

In the tweet, Citron highlights a press release Nvidia released this week. That press release, which covers the launch of Ising, the world's first open AI models "to accelerate the path to useful quantum computers," highlights Infleqtion multiple times.

Infleqtion is listed among the leading quantum enterprises and research labs adopting Ising. The company is also listed for both calibration and decoding, as mentioned in the tweet by Citron.

"The smallest public quantum company by market cap was selected the most by the most important AI infrastructure company on earth," Citron added. "Nvidia ranked these companies for you. The stock market hasn't caught up yet."

Citron said Infleqtion has been selected for the "entire Nvidia quantum stack," while Rigetti is selected for none of it.

"Being selected twice is not a small thing."

What's Next For Infleqtion

Citron has been bullish on Infleqtion stock for months. The stock has struggled since completing its merger in February, with shares down around 6.9%.

Along with the Nvidia news, Citron highlights that Infleqtion had 100% organic growth in 2025 and $550 million in cash with zero debt.

"15 years runway," Citron Research said. "The people who actually understand quantum chose $INFQ."

Citron's post comes after Citigroup recently initiated coverage on Infleqtion stock with a Buy rating and price target of $20.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Corning Stock Is Sliding Today: What's Behind The Drop?
data.description:
• Corning shares are under pressure. What’s pulling GLW shares down?

AI Tailwinds May Already Be Priced In

J.P. Morgan analyst Samik Chatterjee downgraded Corning from Overweight to Neutral, while raising the price target to $175 from $115. The new target implies roughly 7% upside from current levels, but the downgrade reflects concern that the stock's sharp run has outpaced the company's underlying fundamentals.

Chatterjee added that Corning's valuation now  "creates a challenging execution hurdle,” with buy‑side expectations embedding a premium that may be tough for the company to meet.

Despite these wins, J.P. Morgan argued that the market has already priced in much of the AI upside. Chatterjee wrote that investors are increasingly modeling "blue‑sky" scenarios for optical fiber pricing and long‑term scale, leaving little room for disappointment.

GLW Showing Bullish Signs

Corning is still trading near the top of its 52-week range, sitting below the $176.75 high but far above the $40.16 low, which keeps the longer-term trend pointed up even on a down day. The stock is trading 11% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 42.7% above its 100-day SMA, a setup that suggests short-term strength is intact and the intermediate trend remains firmly bullish.

The moving average structure also stays constructive, with the 20-day SMA above the 50-day SMA. The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, is above its signal line with a positive histogram, which leans toward upside momentum still being present despite today's selling.

Over the past 12 months, the stock is up 297.04%, which helps explain why pullbacks can show up quickly as traders lock in gains after a big run. The key question from here is whether the stock can hold above its faster trend lines, because that's where dip-buyers have recently had an edge.

  • Key Support: $152.50 — near the 20-day EMA zone where buyers have tended to step in.
  • Key Resistance: $176.50 — near the prior peak area where rallies have recently stalled.

GLW Shares Slide On Downgrade

GLW Price Action: Corning shares were down 2.83% at $163.49 at the time of publication on Thursday. The stock is trading near its 52-week high of $176.75, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: T. Schneider/Shutterstock
